document.docAuthor: Campello, Lucas, Vaz, Stephanie, Mermudes, Jose R. M., Ferreira, Andre L. D. & Silveira, Luiz F. L.
document.docTitle: Amydetes marolae Campello, Vaz, Mermudes & Silveira 2022, sp. nov.
document.masterDocTitle: Comparative morphology and key to Amydetinae genera, with description of three new firefly species (Coleoptera, Lampyridae)

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Comparative morphology and key to Amydetinae genera, with description of three new firefly species (Coleoptera, Lampyridae)

paragraph.taxonomicName: Amydetes marolae Campello, Vaz, Mermudes & Silveira, 2022

figureCitation.captionText: Figure 9. Amydetes marolae sp. nov., male habitus A ventral B lateral C dorsal. Scale bar: 1 mm (A-C).
Figure 10. Amydetes marolae sp. nov., head A dorsal B ventral C frontal D lateral E posterior F occipital; antennae G dorsal. Scale bars: 1 mm (A-F); 2 mm (G). GB: gular bar.
Figure 11. Amydetes marolae sp. nov., pronotum A dorsal B ventral C lateral D posterior. Scale bar: 1 mm (A-D).
Figure 12. Amydetes marolae sp. nov., aedeagus A dorsal B lateral C ventral; sternum IX D ventral; sternite VIII E ventral; pygidium F dorsal. Scale bars: 0.5 mm (A-D); 1 mm (E, F).

subSubSection.paragraph:
The specific epithet
Amydetes marolae
is a feminine noun in the genitive case. This species is named in honor of our colleague and friend, Raquel Santos Soares Queiroz, alias "Marola" , who helped us collect the type specimens.

caption.paragraph:
Figure 9.
Amydetes marolae
sp. nov., male habitus A ventral B lateral C dorsal. Scale bar: 1 mm ( A-C ).

Figure 10.
Amydetes marolae
sp. nov., head A dorsal B ventral C frontal D lateral E posterior F occipital; antennae G dorsal. Scale bars: 1 mm ( A-F ); 2 mm ( G ). GB : gular bar.

Figure 11.
Amydetes marolae
sp. nov., pronotum A dorsal B ventral C lateral D posterior. Scale bar: 1 mm ( A-D ).

Figure 12.
Amydetes marolae
sp. nov., aedeagus A dorsal B lateral C ventral; sternum IX D ventral; sternite VIII E ventral; pygidium F dorsal. Scale bars: 0.5 mm ( A-D ); 1 mm ( E, F ).

subSubSection.paragraph:
Male. Coloration .
Antennae with scape and pedicel yellowish brown (Fig. 10G ), flagellum pale brown (Fig. 10G ); pronotum yellowish brown (Fig. 11A-D ); abdomen dark brown, with sternites VI- VIII translucent (Figs 9A , 12E ), pygidium dark brown with a translucent posterior line (Fig. 12F ).
Head .
Antennae flabellate with 32-41 antennomeres (Fig. 10G ); antennomere III as long as pedicel, with flabellum 2 x longer than antennomere III (Fig. 10G ). Fronto-clypeus as wide as 1.5 x distance between antennifers process (Fig. 10C ). Maxillary palpomere I as long as wide; II 2 x longer than wide, III approximately 0.1 x of IV length (Fig. 10A-F ). Gular margins separated by 1/3 maxillary palpomere IV length (Fig. 10B ).
Thorax .
Pronotum 1.5 x wider than head width in ventral view (Fig. 9A ), 1.5 x wider than long (Fig. 11A, B ). Hypomeron as long as tall (Fig. 11C ).
Abdomen .
Lanterns occupying almost the entire area of sternite VI and VII (Fig. 9A ). Sternite VIII with posterior margin bisinuate, central 1/3 slightly shorter than posterolateral angles (Fig. 12E ). Pygidium with a translucent posterior line (Fig. 12F ). Phallus 1.5 x longer than parameres (Fig. 12A-C ); parameres basally constricted, as long as phallobase (Fig. 12A ); phallobase asymmetrical (Fig. 12A, C ).

Amydetes marolae
sp. nov. is similar to
A. bellorum
, with which it shares the following combination of traits: labrum connected to fronto-clypeus by membrane; maxillary palpomere IV at least 6 x longer than III (up to 7 x in
A. bellorum
, and at least 10 x in
A. marolae
sp. nov.); hypomeron as long as tall; and sternite VIII bisinuate. Nevertheless,
Amydetes marolae
sp. nov. differs from
A. bellorum
by: body length (average = 0.67 mm [ n = 10, range = 0.6-0.8] in
A. marolae
and 0.87 mm [ n = 10, range = 0.8-1.0] in
A. bellorum
); pygidium (entirely dark brown in
A. bellorum
, dark brown with a translucent posterior line in
A. marolae
sp. nov.).

Amydetes marolae
sp. nov. occurs in a mountainous and coastal region of the Serra do Mar range, where it is found between 160 and 660 m a.s.l., with greater abundance in parts below to 400 m a.s.l. Despite extensive sampling (e.g., Silveira et al. 2020 ),
A. marolae
sp. nov. has never been collected anywhere other than Ilha Grande, unlike
A. bellorum
which does not occur on Ilha Grande. Ilha Grande is separated by approximately 3 km from the mainland, which may be an important barrier to dispersal, especially considering that
Amydetes
females are perhaps flightless ( Silveira and Mermudes 2014a ). As such,
A. marolae
sp. nov. is regarded here as probably endemic to Ilha Grande.
